---
title: "FW 536 — Day 4 morning lab (v3 · marine & stock assessment) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 4 morning lab — Maximum likelihood problem set

**Problem set version:** **v3 — marine & stock assessment theme**.
(The companion version is [v2 — avian / songbird](problem_set_v2.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

> **Note on topic scope.** The morning lecture covers **maximum likelihood**. The Bayesian content comes in the afternoon. The problems below use only what was taught this morning (likelihood ideas, MLE by hand, numerical optimization, mixture models). The remaining problems in `problem_set_v3.html` are reserved for the **afternoon Bayesian lab**.

This template matches [`problem_set_v3.html`](problem_set_v3.html). Write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

---

# Problem 1 — Red snapper post-release survival

> You released 80 red snapper after barotrauma treatment and tracked them with acoustic tags; 47 were detected alive after 7 days. Treat each fish's outcome as Bernoulli($\phi$) with $\phi$ = post-release survival.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 2 — Antarctic krill biomass MLE (log-Normal)

> An acoustic survey gives $n = 18$ density estimates (g/m$^3$) that are strictly positive and right-skewed. You model them as iid Log-Normal: $\log Y_i \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu, \sigma^2)$.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 3 — Beverton–Holt stock–recruitment

> The Beverton–Holt stock-recruitment model is $R = \dfrac{a S}{1 + b S}$ with log-Normal observation error, $\log R_i \sim \mathcal{N}(\log[aS_i/(1+bS_i)], \sigma^2)$. You have 20 (S, R) pairs from a long-term survey.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every morning problem from `problem_set_v3.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
